data.title: Alpha Bank reports FY results
data.description:
• Alpha Bank press release (OTCPK:ALBKY): FY Core PPI broadly flat at €1.3bn (-0.4% Y/Y)
  • NII reached €1.6bn, down -0.7% Y/Y.
  • Normalised Profit After Tax of €196mn in Q4.

data.title: National Bank of Greece reports FY results
data.description:
• National Bank of Greece press release (OTCPK:NBGIF): FY \Group Core PAT1 at €1.3b (+10% yoy), with better-than-expected top line resilience.
  • Recurring OpEx up +5%2 yoy in FY24 reflecting class leading investments in IT and increasing wages. C:CI ratio at 32%, well inside our FY24 target of less than 33%.
  • Core RoTE1 at 17.5% in FY24 before adjusting for excess capital

data.title: TikTok to park $8.8B for data centers in Thailand - report
Biggest stock movers Friday: HPQ, SOUN, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched higher in premarket trading on Friday as investors awaited the January personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index, the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • SoundHound (HOUN) +8% – Shares surged after Q4 results beat estimates, with revenue rising 101% Y/Y. Strong year-end performance propelled the company to the top end of its revenue guidance range, driven by momentum in voice-enabled Agentic AI. SoundHound ended the year with nearly $200 million in cash and no debt. The company raised its full-year 2025 revenue outlook to a range of $157M-$177M (midpoint: $167M), up from the prior $155M-$175M range and above the $165.27M consensus.

Biggest stock losers

  • HP (NYSE:HPQ) -4% – Shares dipped after the company reported FQ1 results, showing a 2% Y/Y decline in its printing segment. For FY2025, HP expects earnings per share between $3.45 and $3.75, with a midpoint of $3.60, above the $3.57 consensus. However, its FQ2 EPS forecast of $0.75 to $0.85, with a midpoint of $0.80, fell short of the $0.86 consensus. The company also stated that by the end of FY2025, over 90% of its products sold in North America will be manufactured outside of China.
  • Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN) -6% – Shares dropped after Q4 results showed a sequential topline decline and a wider loss, along with a weak outlook. The online real estate platform expects Q1 2025 revenue between $1.0B and $1.075B, falling short of the $1.33B consensus. It also forecasts a contribution profit of $40M to $50M, below the $64.4M Visible Alpha estimate, and adjusted EBITDA ranging from -$50M to -$40M, vs. the -$29.3M Visible Alpha estimate.
  • Rocket Lab USA (NASDAQ:RKLB) -13% – Shares plunged on a soft outlook despite a record-setting year, with annual revenue reaching $436.2M, up 78% Y/Y, and a record Q4 revenue of $132.4M, up 121% Y/Y. The company expects Q1 2025 revenue between $117M and $123M, below the $135.67M consensus, and an adjusted EBITDA loss between $33M and $35M.
  • Duolingo (NASDAQ:DUOL) -9% – Shares slipped despite strong results and an upbeat outlook. The language learning platform reported robust user growth, with daily active users surging 51% Y/Y to 40.5M, monthly active users rising 32% to 116.7M, and paid subscribers growing 43% to 9.5M. CEO Luis von Ahn highlighted the success of Duolingo Max, the highest subscription tier, in driving growth. For Q1 2025, the company expects revenue between $220.5M and $223.5M, with a midpoint of $222M, above the $220.76M consensus, while full-year revenue is projected at $962.5M to $978.5M, with a midpoint of $970.5M, exceeding the $964.22M consensus.
  • NetApp (NASDAQ:NTAP) -13% – Shares plunged after the tech firm reported lower-than-expected FQ3 revenue and issued a downbeat outlook. The data management and storage solutions company expects FQ4 adjusted EPS between $1.84 and $1.94, with a midpoint of $1.89, missing the $2.00 estimate. For FY2026, NetApp forecasts adjusted EPS of $7.17 to $7.27, with a midpoint of $7.22, well below the $7.90 consensus.

data.title: International Consolidated Airlines GAAP EPS of €0.557, revenue of €32.1B; initiates outlook
data.description:
• International Consolidated Airlines press release (OTCPK:ICAGY): FY GAAP EPS of €0.557.
  • Revenue of €32.1B (+9.0% Y/Y).
  • Free cash flow of €3,556 million, after investing €2,816 million into the business.
  • Return on invested capital of 17.3%, as a result of disciplined capital allocation.
  • Outlook: to deliver world-class margins and returns • Strong customer demand continues.
  • Non-fuel unit cost trends similar to 2024 (excluding adverse FX impact).
  • Significant free cash flow whilst investing in the business.
  • Sustainable ordinary dividend in place.
  • Excess capital returns to shareholders, reflecting confidence in the outlook.
  • Expecting to deliver sustainable earnings per share growth

data.title: Allianz SE Non-GAAP EPS of €6.31, total business volume of €45.9B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Allianz SE press release (OTCPK:ALIZF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of €6.31.
  • Total business volume of €45.9B (+15.9% Y/Y).
  • Outlook: For 2025, Allianz targets an operating profit of 16.0 billion euros, plus or minus 1 billion euros.
  • Other: Management to propose a dividend per share of 15.40 euros, an increase of 11.6 percent from 2023.
  • A new share buy-back program of up to 2 billion euros has been announced.

data.title: European market dips, auto sector faces pressure
BASF SE reports FY results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• BASF SE press release (OTCQX:BASFY): FY net income came in at €1.3 billion, compared with €225 million in 2023.
  • Net income from shareholdings increased by €798 million to €598 million.
  • Revenue of €65.3B (-5.2% Y/Y).
  • In the 2024 business year, cash flows from operating activities were €6.9 billion, down by €1.2 billion on the prior-year figure. The decline was mainly caused by €1.4 billion less cash released from net working capital.
  • Free cash flow, which remains after deducting payments made for property, plant and equipment and intangible assets from cash flows from operating activities, amounted to €748 million, compared with €2.7 billion in 2023.

  • BASF Group’s outlook for 2025

    In 2025, the BASF Group’s EBITDA before special items is expected to increase to between €8.0 billion and €8.4 billion (2024: €7.9 billion). All segments, with the exception of Chemicals, are likely to contribute to the increase in earnings. In particular, earnings in the Petrochemicals division will be impacted by rising fixed costs in connection with the startup of the new Verbund site in China and scheduled turnarounds.

    The company forecasts the BASF Group’s free cash flow to be between €0.4 billion and €0.8 billion (2024: €0.7 billion). This is based on expected cash flows from operating activities of between €5.6 billion and €6.0 billion, minus expected payments made for property, plant and equipment and intangible assets in the amount of €5.2 billion.

    CO2 emissions are expected to be between 16.7 million metric tons and 17.7 million metric tons in 2025 (2024: 17.0 million metric tons).

data.description:
The unemployment rate in Germany remained unchanged at 6.2% in February.
